message:
perf(core): SimpleProjectedRowidLookup fast path for PK equality SELECT <cols>

The direct-execution fast paths in \`PreparedQueryFastPath\` already
had \`SimpleRowidLookup\` — bypass VDBE for a SELECT-by-primary-key
that returns the whole row. Queries that SELECT only a projected
subset of columns (\`SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE pk = ?\`) did not
match that arm, even though the access pattern is identical: one
rowid, one leaf fetch. Those queries fell back to the VDBE
interpreter, paying a dispatch loop and a full row decode for work
that can be one direct B-tree hit plus a slim projection.

Add a parallel \`SimpleProjectedRowidLookup\` variant that handles
the projected-column case, keeping the full-row arm untouched so
existing workloads do not change shape.

Changes in \`enum PreparedQueryFastPath\`:

- New variant \`SimpleProjectedRowidLookup { root_page,
  parameter_index, rowid_alias_column_index,
  projections: Arc<[PreparedMemValueSource]> }\`. The
  \`projections\` slice is built once at prepare time and captures
  the column sources (column index, coercion, default) that the
  execute path needs to emit each output cell. Held behind Arc so
  the prepared statement caches and re-executes without
  re-deriving the slice.

- \`read_shape()\` returns \"projected_rowid_lookup\" / the
  \"direct_projected_rowid_lookup_query_row\" telemetry tag so
  the new path is individually countable in the fast-path counters.

- The metric bucket reuses
  \`FSQLITE_DIRECT_ROWID_LOOKUP_QUERY_ROW_HITS\` via a combined
  match arm. Projected and full-row PK lookups have the same cost
  shape from the planner's perspective — one leaf fetch — so
  merging the counter keeps downstream dashboards accurate without
  adding a second timeseries.

Changes in \`enum RetainedAutocommitOverlayFastPath\` (the parallel
read-shape enum used by the retained-autocommit overlay):

- \`derive(Copy)\` drops (the new variant holds an
  Arc<[PreparedMemValueSource]>), \`read_shape\`/\`root_page\`
  change from \`self\` to \`&self\`, and a new
  \`ProjectedRowidLookup\` variant mirrors the core-enum variant.
  The overlay path also gets the matching execute wiring so
  retained-autocommit transactions don't fall off the fast path
  for projected SELECTs.

Row materialization:

- Two helpers, \`row_from_memdb_value_sources\` and
  \`row_from_txn_value_sources\`, take
  \`projections: &[PreparedMemValueSource]\` and a source row and
  build the projected row without going through the VDBE column
  emitter. Both arms of the execute dispatch
  (\`PreparedQueryFastPath\` and \`RetainedAutocommitOverlayFastPath\`)
  now call these helpers.

Prepare-time wiring:

- New helper
  \`prepared_simple_rowid_lookup_projection_sources(columns, table,
  table_label)\` walks the \`SELECT\` column list, resolves each
  reference against the table schema, and produces the
  \`PreparedMemValueSource\` slice (or bails, returning
  \`None\`, if any column isn't a simple direct projection).
- \`prepared_simple_rowid_lookup_fast_path\` is extended: when the
  query shape matches rowid-equality and the projected list passes
  the helper above, it emits the new
  \`SimpleProjectedRowidLookup\` variant instead of falling back
  to VDBE. The full-\`SELECT *\` case still emits the existing
  \`SimpleRowidLookup\` variant.

Tests added, each locking a distinct contract:

- \`test_prepare_projected_primary_key_lookup_arms_query_fast_path\`
  — prepare time: asserts
  \`PreparedQueryFastPath::SimpleProjectedRowidLookup\` was
  selected for the projected SELECT, so the matcher in
  prepared_simple_rowid_lookup_fast_path stays wired.
- \`test_prepared_projected_primary_key_lookup_query_uses_fast_path\`
  — run time: asserts the prepared statement, when executed,
  surfaces the fast path in its per-statement metadata and not
  VDBE fallback.
- \`test_file_backed_prepared_projected_rowid_lookup_uses_retained_overlay_without_flush\`
  — retained-autocommit overlay hit: asserts the overlay variant
  serves the read without flushing pending writes to the file-
  backed store.
- \`test_memory_retained_autocommit_projected_rowid_lookup_uses_overlay_without_flush\`
  — same assertion on the memory-backed store, pins that both
  storage backends share the fast path.

Pure additive perf lever. No SQL semantics, result shape, or error
handling changes.
